title: Burn Bags - FBI

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Burn Bags - FBI
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

FOIA REQUEST

Federal Bureau of Investigation
Records/Information Dissemination Section 

Freedom of Information Act Request

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request access to and copies of the following records:

Requested Records

Any and all records from August , 2025 to present relating to the discovery, recovery, inventorying, preservation, transfer, review, or handling of “burn bags” or materials reportedly located within a secure room, SCIF, vault, archive area, or undisclosed records storage location within FBI facilities containing records associated with:

“Crossfire Hurricane”
the Trump–Russia investigation
the Durham investigation or Durham annex
Russiagate-related records
classified annex materials reportedly referenced by FBI Director Kash Patel
records provided or transmitted to Senator Chuck Grassley or the Senate Judiciary Committee

This request includes but is not limited to:

emails
memoranda
inventories
chain-of-custody records
photographs
preservation notices
records destruction suspensions
internal FBI communications 
records referencing “burn bags”
records discussing the existence or discovery of previously undisclosed document repositories
communications with DOJ, ODNI, CIA, NSA, or Congress regarding the records
briefing materials prepared for FBI leadership
records discussing declassification review of any recovered materials

Search Terms

Please include searches reasonably calculated to locate responsive records using terms including:

“burn bag”
“Crossfire Hurricane”
“Durham annex”
“secret room”
“SCIF”
“Grassley”
“Russiagate”
“hidden documents”
“classified annex”
“Kash Patel”

Public Interest

This request concerns matters of exceptional public interest involving alleged handling, preservation, and possible destruction of historically significant FBI and intelligence-community records tied to the origins of the Trump–Russia investigation. Media reports indicate these materials may involve classified annexes and records previously unknown to Congress or the public.

Format Requested

I request responsive records in electronic format pursuant to the E-FOIA amendments, preferably as searchable PDF files delivered via email.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a waiver of all search and duplication fees because disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and activities. I am a requester engaged in news media and public-interest research activities.

However, if fees are expected to exceed $25, please contact me before processing.

Related recent news:  https://www.dailymail.com/news/article-15795357/The-FBI-hidden-burn-bag-room-Kash-Patel.html

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes

title: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DC ID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DC ID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

SUBJECT: Freedom of Information Act Request: DISB Consumer Complaint DCID #2059580 (Shahidah Williams)

To the FOIA Officer, Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking (DISB):

Pursuant to the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am requesting expedited access to the following public records regarding a consumer complaint I filed against City Lending, Inc. in the fall of 2019. 

The complaint was assigned DCID #2059580 and was handled by Consumer Services Specialist Shahidah S. Williams, JD, within the Compliance Analysis Division. 

On November 6, 2019, Ms. Williams stated in writing that she was administratively closing my complaint and promised that an "official letter" would be generated. However, the core issues of predatory payoff escalations and the withholding of monthly statements were never resolved by your agency.

I demand the immediate production of the following precise records generated between September 1, 2019, and December 31, 2019:

1. The Official Closeout Letter: The formal administrative closeout letter referenced by Shahidah Williams on November 6, 2019, regarding the termination of complaint DCID #2059580.
2. Investigative Notes & Justification: All internal memoranda, case notes, or communications authored by Shahidah Williams justifying the closure of this complaint while severe discrepancies remained between the payoff provided by City Lending and the escalated payoff provided by Samuel I. White, P.C.
3. Supervisory Authorization: All records showing supervisory review and approval authorizing Shahidah Williams to administratively close DCID #2059580.
4. City Lending Communications: All emails, letters, and notes of telephone calls between Shahidah Williams and any representative of City Lending, Inc. (specifically Jorge Campodonico or Stephanie Simmons) regarding their failure to provide the consumer with monthly billing statements and their refusal to update the consumer's mailing address.
5. Escalation & Disciplinary Action (Predatory Payoff): All records, internal memoranda, and enforcement referrals generated by Shahidah Williams, her supervisors, or the Compliance Analysis Division detailing how the initial fabricated payoff amounts submitted by City Lending were escalated for regulatory review, and any subsequent disciplinary or enforcement actions taken against the lender.

I request these records be provided in their native, electronic format with all metadata intact. Because these records involve documented regulatory abandonment and subsequent financial harm to a District resident, I expect a comprehensive search that goes beyond a surface-level query.

Please provide a Vaughn Index for any documents or portions of documents that you claim are exempt from disclosure. 

Sincerely,

Fred Gamble Jr.
1490 Morris Rd SE
Washington, DC 20020

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

title: Flock Raven (Ventura Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Raven records created by the department and Flock Safety
Databases, spreadsheets, and any other documents that provide the following information collectively or individually:
- Number of alerts
- Description, details about the alert (Examples: gunfire, car crash, distress, sideshow)

List of agencies your department shares access with and agencies sharing their Raven's with your department

title: Ted Turner (CNN)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Ted Turner (CNN)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Cable News Network founder Robert Edward "Ted" Turner III (November 19, 1938 – May 6, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/06/business/media/ted-turner-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: Request for Clarification - AF 2026-04794-F
communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your May 6, 2026, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for "all documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Air Force and the production of the upcoming film "First to Orion," directed by Wes Miller." (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2015 To 05/06/2026).  We have assigned number 2026-04794-F to identify your request. 
Our agency is committed to processing your request in a timely and complete manner. However, we have determined that your request, as currently written, is too broad in scope to allow for a meaningful search for records. The FOIA requires that a request must "reasonably describe" the records sought (5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A)). Requests that are vague or seek 'any and all documents,' 'any documents,' or 'all documents' are considered impermissibly broad and do not meet this statutory requirement to 'reasonably describe such records', as they would require an unreasonably burdensome search. (See Exxon Mobil Corp. v. U.S. Dep't of Interior ). 
To proceed, we require your assistance in narrowing the scope of your request. A focused request enables us to conduct an efficient, targeted search and provide you with relevant records more quickly. To help us process your request, please provide more detail on the following: 

 Specific Document Types: Please advise what specific Air Force documents you are seeking (Generally, a record is reasonably described when the description contains sufficient file-related information (type of document, title, subject area, date of creation, originator, etc.); or the request contains enough event-related information (date and circumstances surrounding the event the record covers) to permit the conduct of an organized, non-random search.) 
 Specific Time Frame: Please provide a specific date range for the records you are seeking (e.g., January 1, 2020, to December 31, 2023). A request without a time frame or in this case a 10 year time frame is generally considered overly broad. 
 Email Correspondence: For any part of your request involving emails, please provide: 
 Custodians: The names or job titles of the individuals whose email accounts you believe should be searched. 
 Senders/Recipients: The names or email addresses for the "To," "From," "CC," or "BCC" fields. 
 Keywords: Specific search terms to be used within the body or subject line of the emails. Please note that the FOIA does not require agencies to answer questions or interrogatories; analyze and/or interpret documents for a requester; create records; conduct research; initiate investigations; or provide statutes, regulations, publications, or other documents that are otherwise made available to the public.  Requests only apply to records in existence at the time your request is received. 
Providing us with these details is crucial as it permits us to conduct an efficient search, allowing our staff to conduct a focused search within the appropriate databases and systems.  It provided for efficient resource allocation, which helps us dedicate our resources effectively to locating records that directly correspond to your request.  Finally, it allows us to provide a more timely response. 
If we do not receive the requested clarification by 19 June 2026, your request will be administratively closed. If you are dissatisfied with the servicing of this request or in this response from this office, you may contact the Air Force FOIA Public Liaison Officer, Mr. Robert Bivins, concerning this response at AF FOIA Public Liaison Office (SAF/CNZA), 1800 Air Force Pentagon, Washington, DC 20330-1800, DAF.FOIA@US.AF.MIL or (703) 614-8500. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at OGIS@NARA.GOV; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. 
We appreciate your understanding and cooperation in this matter. Please direct your questions to SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.WORKFLOW@US.AF.MIL and use this assigned number, 2026-04794-F, to assist us in responding more promptly. Very Respectfully, SAF/AAGI FOIA TEAM Government Information Management Branch (SAF/AAGI) Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000

communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your May 6, 2026, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request fo"all documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Air Force and the production of a film or TV program known as "Baby Shower."" Our agency is committed to processing your request in a timely and complete manner. However, we have determined that your request, as currently written, is too broad in scope to allow for a meaningful search for records. The FOIA requires that a request must "reasonably describe" the records sought (5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A)). Requests that are vague or seek 'any and all documents,' 'any documents,' or 'all documents' are considered impermissibly broad and do not meet this statutory requirement to 'reasonably describe such records', as they would require an unreasonably burdensome search. (See Exxon Mobil Corp. v. U.S. Dep't of Interior ). 
To proceed, we require your assistance in narrowing the scope of your request. A focused request enables us to conduct an efficient, targeted search and provide you with relevant records more quickly. To help us process your request, please provide more detail on the following: 
 Specific Document Types: Please advise what specific Air Force documents you are seeking (Generally, a record is reasonably described when the description contains sufficient file-related information (type of document, title, subject area, date of creation, originator, etc.); or the request contains enough event-related information (date and circumstances surrounding the event the record covers) to permit the conduct of an organized, non-random search.) 
 Specific Time Frame: Please provide a specific date range for the records you are seeking (e.g., January 1, 2020, to December 31, 2023). A request without a time frame or in this case a 10 year time frame is generally considered overly broad. 
 Email Correspondence: For any part of your request involving emails, please provide: 
 Custodians: The names or job titles of the individuals whose email accounts you believe should be searched. 
 Senders/Recipients: The names or email addresses for the "To," "From," "CC," or "BCC" fields. 
 Keywords: Specific search terms to be used within the body or subject line of the emails. 
Please note that the FOIA does not require agencies to answer questions or interrogatories; analyze and/or interpret documents for a requester; create records; conduct research; initiate investigations; or provide statutes, regulations, publications, or other documents that are otherwise made available to the public.  Requests only apply to records in existence at the time your request is received.

communications.subject: Request Acknowledgement - AF 2026-04789-F
communications.communication:
Dear Brett  Carollo, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your May 6, 2026, Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for "the 2016 annual budget report for the Public Affairs Agency of the U.S. Air Force." (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2016 To 05/06/2026). Based on our initial review, we believe we cannot process your request within the statutory 20 workdays because our organization has a significant number of pending FOIA requests.  To ensure all requesters are properly served in the order their requests were received, we have instituted multitrack processing of requests.  Based on the information you provided, we have placed your request in the complex track. The Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) provides for the collection of fees based on the costs of processing a FOIA request and your fee category.  Based upon the information provided in your request, we have placed you in the Other category.  Regarding your request for a fee waiver, we defer regarding this, or any subsequent fee issue until the review of responsive records is complete.  At that time you will be contacted relative to fees. We have assigned number 2026-04789-F to identify your request; should you need to contact us about your request.  Please direct your questions to SAF.AA.HAF.FOIA.Workflow@us.af.mil and use this assigned number to assist us in responding more promptly. We will strive to provide an update between 90 and 120 days.  Our policy is to process requests within their respective tracks in the order in which they were received in accordance with 32 CFR, Part 286.8.  We will notify you in writing if additional processing time is required.  We do process each FOIA request as quickly as possible, however delayed responses can be expected. Very Respectfully, SAF/AAGI FOIA TEAM Government Information Management Branch (SAF/AAGI) Office of the Secretary of the Air Force 1000 Air Force Pentagon Washington, DC 20330-1000
